title: Backend test gaps: availability/audit/api-tokens coverage
description:
Review found concrete gaps:

Availability routes (apps/api/src/routes/availability.ts)
- Missing handler integration tests for weekly rules: listRules, getRule, createRule, updateRule, deleteRule, setWeeklyAvailability (happy path + pagination + NOT_FOUND + RLS isolation + validation errors).
- Missing tests for overrides: listOverrides, getOverride, createOverride, updateOverride, deleteOverride (validate date-range logic; RLS; NOT_FOUND).
- Missing tests for blocked time: listBlockedTime, getBlockedTime, createBlockedTime, updateBlockedTime, deleteBlockedTime including Date conversion (startAt/endAt string -> Date), optional fields in update, and recurringRule.
- Missing tests for scheduling limits CRUD: listSchedulingLimits, getSchedulingLimits, createSchedulingLimits, updateSchedulingLimits, deleteSchedulingLimits (validate allowed combos; RLS).
- Engine endpoints lack route-level coverage: getDates, getTimes, checkSlot (validate schemas, Date conversion for checkSlot, and errors bubble up when calendar not linked).
- Feed endpoint only has export test; needs HTTP or handler test for GET /availability/feed with availabilityFeedQuerySchema and response validation (availabilityFeedResponseSchema.parse).
- Router coverage gap: uiRouter exposes availability full suite while apiRouter exposes only engine; add tests ensuring only engine routes exist in apiRouter and auth differences are enforced (authed vs adminOnly).

Audit routes (apps/api/src/routes/audit.ts)
- No tests for list/get (adminOnly).
- List filters need coverage: entityType, entityId, actorId, action, startDate, endDate, pagination (limit+1, nextCursor) and Luxon day-boundary logic.
- get returns null (not error) when missing; verify behavior.

API token routes (apps/api/src/routes/api-tokens.ts)
- No tests for list, get, create, update, revoke (adminOnly).
- Coverage should include: token creation returns raw token once; stored hash + tokenPrefix format; list excludes revoked unless includeRevoked; update/revoke throw BAD_REQUEST when already revoked and NOT_FOUND when missing; updatedAt/revokedAt set; RLS scoping enforced.

Other
- Health test is config-only stub (apps/api/src/health.test.ts); add HTTP-level health check once test server harness is available.
- Add auth-context negative tests using createUnauthenticatedContext/createTokenContext where appropriate (e.g., adminOnly reject).
close_reason: Added coverage for availability, audit, and api token routes

title: Blocker: Fix schedule range contract + client history count types
description:
Blocker details:
- /appointments/range contract: appointmentTimeRangeQuerySchema includes limit (default 500), but appointmentTimeRangeResponseSchema has only items with no hasMore/nextCursor. Service uses list() with limit and drops pagination metadata, so schedule views can silently miss appointments when > limit. Fix: either remove limit/guarantee complete range OR add cursor metadata and wire through to response.
  Files: packages/dto/src/schemas/appointment.ts:72-105, apps/api/src/routes/appointments.ts:29-50, apps/api/src/services/appointments.ts:122-154.
- Client history summary counts: repo uses count(*) aggregates which are BIGINT in Postgres (often returned as string by drivers). DTO expects number via nonNegativeIntSchema (no coercion) and route parses result, so zod can throw at runtime. Fix: cast counts to int in SQL, coerce in DTO, or normalize in repo.
  Files: apps/api/src/repositories/clients.ts:139-150, packages/dto/src/schemas/client.ts:46-56, apps/api/src/routes/clients.ts:73-83.
design: Keep contract shapes consistent with UX expectations: schedule range must be complete or explicitly paginated; history summary should be type-safe regardless of driver numeric coercion. Prefer explicitness over implicit driver behavior.
acceptance_criteria:
- /appointments/range returns either a complete set for the requested range (no silent truncation) or includes pagination metadata (hasMore/nextCursor) and clients can request additional pages.
- Client history summary counts validate reliably (no runtime Zod parse failures) via SQL cast, DTO coercion, or repository normalization.
- Tests or validation updated to cover these cases.
notes: Context: DTO + route contracts for schedule range and client history were added. Current implementation can silently truncate schedule data and can throw on history summary parse when counts are bigint/string.
close_reason: Completed

title: Adopt split-pane layout in Calendars list
description: Replace the drawer-based Calendars list/detail UX with the new split-pane components (SplitPaneLayout, ListPanel, DetailPanel, DetailTabs) from apps/admin-ui/src/components/split-pane.tsx. Update apps/admin-ui/src/routes/_authenticated/calendars/index.tsx to render a two-pane layout with list on the left and details on the right, and open the detail panel when a row is selected. Use query params (selected, tab) to persist selection and tab state. On mobile, details should open in the sheet from DetailPanel. Remove usage of CalendarDrawer in this route once split pane is wired. Acceptance: list loads with no layout regressions, selecting a calendar shows details in the right panel (desktop) and sheet (mobile), tabs switch correctly, and browser back clears selection. Add a short note in the route code about replacing drawer usage.
close_reason: Implemented split-pane Calendars layout

title: Fix availability overlap queries for blocked time + appointments
description: Availability engine loads existing appointments and blocked time by startAt/endAt within range, missing records that overlap the range (start before/end after). This can mark slots available when they are not. Update availability repository queries to use overlap predicates (start_at < range_end AND end_at > range_start), including long blocks that fully span the range.
notes:
Additional review findings to address:
- Resource capacity check ignores other appointment types that share a resource, so capacity can be exceeded when overlapping different types.
- Availability engine only uses the first weekly rule per weekday; additional rules are ignored despite being allowed/returned elsewhere.
- Global scheduling limits may be skipped because calendarId NULL is checked with eq(undefined), so defaults may not apply.
close_reason: Fix overlap predicates, weekday rules, and resource capacity

title: Integrate keyboard shortcuts into calendars split-pane UI
description:
## Summary
QA testing revealed that keyboard shortcuts for focus zones and list navigation are defined in hooks but not integrated into the calendars page UI.

## Issues Found

### 1. Focus Zone IDs Missing
The split-pane components (`ListPanel`, `DetailPanel`) don't set the `id` attributes needed for `useFocusZones` to work.

**Expected:** Cmd+L focuses list, Cmd+D focuses detail, Cmd+F focuses filter
**Actual:** These shortcuts do nothing because DOM elements lack required IDs

**Files:**
- `apps/admin-ui/src/components/split-pane.tsx` - needs to accept/set focus zone IDs
- `apps/admin-ui/src/routes/_authenticated/calendars/index.tsx` - needs to call `useFocusZones`

### 2. List Navigation Not Integrated
The `useListNavigation` hook exists but is not used in the calendars page.

**Expected:** j/k/arrows navigate list, Enter opens selected item
**Actual:** No keyboard navigation in table

**Files:**
- `apps/admin-ui/src/routes/_authenticated/calendars/index.tsx` - needs to integrate `useListNavigation`

### 3. Navigation Shortcuts Not Firing Outside Command Palette
The `g d`, `g c` etc. shortcuts defined in `useNavigationShortcuts` show in command palette but don't respond to direct keyboard input.

**Expected:** Pressing `g` then `d` navigates to Dashboard
**Actual:** Key sequence has no effect

**Files:**
- `apps/admin-ui/src/hooks/use-keyboard-shortcuts.ts` - may need debugging
- `apps/admin-ui/src/routes/__root.tsx` - calls `useNavigationShortcuts()`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Cmd+L focuses first focusable element in list panel
- [ ] Cmd+D focuses first focusable element in detail panel  
- [ ] Cmd+F focuses filter/search input (if present)
- [ ] Escape closes detail panel or blurs focused input
- [ ] j/ArrowDown selects next item in list
- [ ] k/ArrowUp selects previous item in list
- [ ] Enter opens selected item
- [ ] g d, g c, g a etc. navigation shortcuts work from keyboard

## Technical Approach
1. Update `ListPanel` and `DetailPanel` to accept optional `id` prop, defaulting to FOCUS_ZONES constants
2. Add `useFocusZones({ onEscape: clearDetails, detailOpen })` call to calendars page
3. Track selected index state and integrate `useListNavigation` with calendar items
4. Debug why key sequences in `useNavigationShortcuts` aren't triggering

## QA Evidence
Screenshots saved at:
- /tmp/qa-01-dashboard.png through /tmp/qa-08-final.png
- URL params verified working: `?selected=<uuid>&tab=<value>`
- Back/forward navigation verified working
- Command palette (Cmd+K) verified working
close_reason: Implemented keyboard shortcuts integration: useFocusZones for Cmd+L/D/Escape, useListNavigation for j/k/arrows. Verified working with agent-browser testing.

title: Fix useScheduleAppointments hook to use correct API parameters
description:
Fix the schedule view API parameter mismatch causing 400 errors.

**Problem:**
The `useScheduleAppointments` hook in `apps/admin-ui/src/hooks/use-schedule-appointments.ts` sends:
- `startAt: weekStart.toISOString()`
- `endAt: weekEnd.toISOString()`

But `orpc.appointments.list` expects:
- `startDate: 'YYYY-MM-DD'`
- `endDate: 'YYYY-MM-DD'`

**Solution:**
Change the hook to use the correct parameter names and format:
```typescript
const input = useMemo(() => ({
  limit: 200,
  startDate: formatDateParam(weekStart),  // YYYY-MM-DD format
  endDate: formatDateParam(weekEnd),      // YYYY-MM-DD format
  // ... filters
}), [...]);
```

**Files to modify:**
- `apps/admin-ui/src/hooks/use-schedule-appointments.ts`

**Verification:**
- Appointments should appear in schedule grid on correct day/time
- No 400 errors in console when viewing schedule
close_reason: Fixed: Updated useScheduleAppointments hook and schedule-grid component to use appointments.range endpoint

title: Schedule view uses wrong API parameters (startAt/endAt vs startDate/endDate)
description:
The useScheduleAppointments hook calls orpc.appointments.list with startAt/endAt (ISO timestamps) but the listAppointmentsQuerySchema expects startDate/endDate (YYYY-MM-DD date strings). This causes 400 errors and appointments don't appear in schedule view.

Fix: Either change the hook to use startDate/endDate format, or change it to call appointments.range endpoint which accepts startAt/endAt timestamps.
close_reason: Fixed: Changed orpc.appointments.list to orpc.appointments.range and updated ScheduleAppointment interface to match range endpoint response
